text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint coastal village nestled near where pristine tide pools kissed the shore, lived a charming fox named Felix. Felix was no ordinary fox—he possessed a mop of russet fur, cunning smarts, and an irrepressible sense of humor.

On a particularly bustling Saturday, Felix decided he wanted ice cream. Now, Felix was no gourmet connoisseur. He'd eat anything sweet and cold that came his way. So he dashed off through the village, zigzagging past the seaside bakery where Mrs. Buttercup always made her famous chocolateice churned treats. He barreled past the strawberry ice cream parlor where Benny was busy perfecting his technique for serving it from a bowl. Despite their delicious fruits, Felix never indulged—his palette was quite basic.

Far past the familiar sights, Felix discovered a forgotten path behind an old oak tree. It was quiet there, just him and the lazy songbirds. He darted along, scooping up mouth
